Oracle Events
OE1: Look up Fatimah Al-Rashidi's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Fatimah" or "Al-Rashidi") to confirm her email address (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) and role (Relocation Coordinator). This establishes the persona identity and the sender address for any follow-up emails.
OE2: Search the Airtable Relocations table using airtable_list_records (base_id: "base_moveops_ops", table_name: "Relocations") or airtable_search_records (base_id: "base_moveops_ops", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Fatimah") to find all relocations assigned to Fatimah. The agent should discover 6 active (In Progress) relocations: (1) Tomás Herrera, GreenStack Energy, Chicago → Austin, April 14–16; (2) Lena Kowalski, Mosaic Robotics, Detroit → Salt Lake City, April 22–24; (3) Dr. Renata Kovacs, Canopy Health, Nashville → Toronto, April 14–17; (4) Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Houston → Portland, April 17–28; (5) Nadia Okonjo, PivotPoint Labs, San Francisco → Austin, April 14–18; (6) Raj Patel, NorthWind Technologies, Chicago → Denver (recRajPatelAuditNote0411) — Special Requirements note that movers arrived at 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM IT escort and loaded servers without waiting, indicating a chain-of-custody breach. The Mei-Lin Zhao record has Special Requirements flagging HAZMAT: Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules (~350 lbs), UN3481-certified packaging, climate-controlled transport 15–25°C, DOT hazmat shipping permit needed, and split shipment required. Note: Dr. Anand Mehta does NOT have an Airtable relocation record — his relocation is tracked through Linear tickets (discovered in OE5) and emails (discovered in OE3).
OE3: Search emails using search_emails with queries for each relocatee name (e.g., "Mei-Lin Zhao", "Kovacs", "Nadia Okonjo", "Mehta", "Herrera", "Kowalski", "Raj Patel") as well as "hazmat" or "DOT" to find status, pending items, and deadlines across all relocations. Key discoveries include: (a) Mei-Lin Zhao — Derek Hollis's email details Class 9 lithium-ion hazmat requirements (UN3481, climate-controlled, split shipment), Hana Kim's invoice email (INV-2026-0418) shows $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge + $150 interim hazmat storage; Houston lab lease hard-ends April 17, Portland lab not ready until April 28 creating an 11-day interim storage gap requiring DOT-compliant temperature-controlled facility; (b) Dr. Kovacs — employer letter from Canopy HR needed by April 7 for customs documentation (now overdue), $850 customs broker surcharge pending client approval from Patricia Langford; (c) Nadia Okonjo — H-1B visa holder, USCIS address change must be coordinated with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) before the physical move, building super requires 48-hour advance notice and weekdays-only moves; (d) Dr. Mehta — UrbanNest housing options forwarded, Dr. Mehta has until end of day April 25 to select from the three furnished temporary housing options (per Fatimah's email forwarding UrbanNest's River North/Streeterville/Gold Coast options), equipment inventory confirmed (Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet with chain-of-custody requirements); (e) Raj Patel — chain-of-custody concerns from the NorthWind relocation, Sentinel Chain Logistics dispatch email to Fatimah about the supervised load incident; (f) PHMSA formal compliance inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) from Linda Castellano, requesting documentation for all hazmat shipments, deadline extended to June 5.
OE4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "PHMSA" or "compliance") to find the DOT compliance audit context and the hazmat training details. Key discoveries: (a) Chloe Vance's message requesting all coordinators review their in-progress moves for anything DOT-regulated and send details before Thursday's training session; (b) Elena Rostova's new mandatory DOT documentation policy (effective immediately) requiring a completed DOT Documentation Checklist before any hazmat relocation can be marked Completed or invoiced; (c) Chloe's hazmat documentation checklist covering 4 categories: shipping papers, hazmat placarding certificates, carrier DOT/PHMSA certifications, and emergency response documentation; (d) DOT compliance audit status across 4 hazmat moves (Mei-Lin Zhao/GreenStack = COMPLETE, Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic = missing signed cert, Raj Patel/NorthWind = COMPLETE, Joaquín Reyes/GreenStack = COMPLETE); (e) Emeka Diallo's Slack message flagging that both GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald and Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick are Seattle/April moves coordinated by Fatimah, warning to keep them straight.
OE5: Search Linear for Fatimah's assigned issues using linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi") and also search the DOT compliance project using linear_list_issues (project: "project_dot_compliance_audit") to find ticket statuses and existing documentation efforts. Discoveries: (a) Fatimah's active tickets — Noah Fitzgerald: apartment (issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_apt, in_progress), flight (issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_flight, todo), movers (issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_movers, todo); Dr. Anand Mehta: temporary housing (linear_issue_bd446671ceed, in_progress), equipment chain-of-custody (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a, in_progress, due April 30), household move (linear_issue_4902f5c68acb, in_progress, due May 3). Note: Dr. Mehta's relocation is discovered here through Linear tickets assigned to Fatimah, even though he has no Airtable relocation record; (b) DOT Compliance Audit project — 4 DOT docs tickets assigned to Chloe (moveops_chloe_vance): Zhao/GreenStack (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, done), Tanaka/Mosaic (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic, in_progress), Patel/NorthWind (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, done), Reyes/GreenStack (issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack, done); plus a subtask for obtaining the retroactive placarding certificate from Swift (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, todo); (c) No dedicated Linear tickets exist for Dr. Kovacs, Nadia Okonjo, Tomás Herrera, or Lena Kowalski relocations.
OE6: Add a comment to the existing DOT compliance ticket for Mei-Lin Zhao (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack) or to another relevant Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack" or a relocation-specific ticket, body: note identifying Mei-Lin Zhao's move as DOT-regulated with Class 9 lithium-ion battery modules under UN3481, and listing documentation requirements such as shipping papers, hazmat placarding certificate, carrier DOT/PHMSA certification, and emergency response documentation). This is a WRITE ACTION.
OE7: Look up contact information for follow-up email recipients. For Patricia Langford, use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") to find her email address patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io. For Lorraine Pham, her contact is NOT in the Contacts database — her email (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) must be discovered through the Airtable relocation record for Nadia Okonjo (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin, which lists the attorney details in Special Requirements), or through emails in the PivotPoint relocation thread.
OE8: Send a follow-up email for Dr. Kovacs's relocation using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"], subject: relating to Dr. Kovacs employer letter or documentation follow-up, content: referencing the needed employer letter confirming end of US employment and new Canadian role, and/or the pending $850 customs broker surcharge approval needed before MoveOps can finalize the vendor). This is a WRITE ACTION.
OE9: Send a follow-up email for Nadia Okonjo's relocation using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com"] or another relevant contact such as sam@pivotpointlabs.com, subject: relating to Nadia Okonjo H-1B address change or USCIS coordination, content: referencing the need to confirm USCIS address-change filing is handled before the physical move date of April 14–18, and/or the 48-hour building super notice requirement). This is a WRITE ACTION.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE1: Look up Fatimah Al-Rashidi's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Fatimah" or "Al-Rashidi") to confirm her email address (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) and role (Relocation Coordinator). This establishes the persona identity and the sender address for any follow-up emails.

The agent should discover 6 active (In Progress) relocations: (1) Tomás Herrera, GreenStack Energy, Chicago → Austin, April 14–16; (2) Lena Kowalski, Mosaic Robotics, Detroit → Salt Lake City, April 22–24; (3) Dr. Renata Kovacs, Canopy Health, Nashville → Toronto, April 14–17; (4) Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Houston → Portland, April 17–28; (5) Nadia Okonjo, PivotPoint Labs, San Francisco → Austin, April 14–18; (6) Raj Patel, NorthWind Technologies, Chicago → Denver (recRajPatelAuditNote0411) — Special Requirements note that movers arrived at 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM IT escort and loaded servers without waiting, indicating a chain-of-custody breach. The Mei-Lin Zhao record has Special Requirements flagging HAZMAT: Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules (~350 lbs), UN3481-certified packaging, climate-controlled transport 15–25°C, DOT hazmat shipping permit needed, and split shipment required. Note: Dr. Anand Mehta does NOT have an Airtable relocation record — his relocation is tracked through Linear tickets (discovered in OE5) and emails (discovered in OE3).
OE3: Search emails using search_emails with queries for each relocatee name (e.g., "Mei-Lin Zhao", "Kovacs", "Nadia Okonjo", "Mehta", "Herrera", "Kowalski", "Raj Patel") as well as "hazmat" or "DOT" to find status, pending items, and deadlines across all relocations. Key discoveries include: (a) Mei-Lin Zhao — Derek Hollis's email details Class 9 lithium-ion hazmat requirements (UN3481, climate-controlled, split shipment), Hana Kim's invoice email (INV-2026-0418) shows $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge + $150 interim hazmat storage; Houston lab lease hard-ends April 17, Portland lab not ready until April 28 creating an 11-day interim storage gap requiring DOT-compliant temperature-controlled facility; (b) Dr. Kovacs — employer letter from Canopy HR needed by April 7 for customs documentation (now overdue), $850 customs broker surcharge pending client approval from Patricia Langford; (c) Nadia Okonjo — H-1B visa holder, USCIS address change must be coordinated with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) before the physical move, building super requires 48-hour advance notice and weekdays-only moves; (d) Dr. Mehta — UrbanNest housing options forwarded, Dr. Mehta has until end of day April 25 to select from the three furnished temporary housing options (per Fatimah's email forwarding UrbanNest's River North/Streeterville/Gold Coast options), equipment inventory confirmed (Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet with chain-of-custody requirements); (e) Raj Patel — chain-of-custody concerns from the NorthWind relocation, Sentinel Chain Logistics dispatch email to Fatimah about the supervised load incident; (f) PHMSA formal compliance inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) from Linda Castellano, requesting documentation for all hazmat shipments, deadline extended to June 5.
OE4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "PHMSA" or "compliance") to find the DOT compliance audit context and the hazmat training details. Key discoveries: (a) Chloe Vance's message requesting all coordinators review their in-progress moves for anything DOT-regulated and send details before Thursday's training session; (b) Elena Rostova's new mandatory DOT documentation policy (effective immediately) requiring a completed DOT Documentation Checklist before any hazmat relocation can be marked Completed or invoiced; (c) Chloe's hazmat documentation checklist covering 4 categories: shipping papers, hazmat placarding certificates, carrier DOT/PHMSA certifications, and emergency response documentation; (d) DOT compliance audit status across 4 hazmat moves (Mei-Lin Zhao/GreenStack = COMPLETE, Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic = missing signed cert, Raj Patel/NorthWind = COMPLETE, Joaquín Reyes/GreenStack = COMPLETE); (e) Emeka Diallo's Slack message flagging that both GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald and Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick are Seattle/April moves coordinated by Fatimah, warning to keep them straight.

Clearly references Mei-Lin Zhao's hazmat shipment.

It does not reference Mei-Lin Zhao's Class 9 lithium-ion battery modules. The agent created new Linear issues (#483, #484) for Zhao and Patel rather than adding comments to existing Zhao tickets.
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment call on issue 'issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack' explicitly states in its body: 'Mei-Lin Zhao / Class 9 lithium-ion / UN3481' and details Class 9 shipping papers with UN3481 designation. ✓Run #6The linear_create_comment call for issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack explicitly includes 'Material: Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules (~350 lbs, 4 crated modules), UN3481' in the body, directly referencing Zhao's hazmat shipment as DOT-regulated.
